title: codiaeum hawkeri
intro: Codiaeum hawkeri, commonly known as the Hawker's croton, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is known for its vibrant, colorful leaves that display a range of patterns and colors, making it a popular ornamental plant. Codiaeum hawkeri has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include providing relief from stress and anxiety through its adaptogenic effects, and it has been used to support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Codiaeum hawkeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic actions.

side_effects: Codiaeum hawkeri can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and even digestive issues in some individuals. The plant contains toxic compounds called anthraquinones, which can cause symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea if ingested. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also lead to eye irritation, redness, and even temporary blindness. If ingested in large quantities, codiaeum hawkeri can cause more severe side effects, including liver and kidney damage.
